stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 88.7 psi and a standard deviation of 9.1 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.0% and 5.9%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 88.7 psi. After correcting for the 4.5% bias: ≈ 92.9 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.0%-5.9%) and sampling variability: [88.1, 97.6]. Point estimate: 92.9 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
